title: Chewing Gum on Carpet
stainChemistry: Same hydrophobic polymer as gum-cotton. On carpet, the key rule is the same: FREEZE to brittle before touching it. Ice pack in a plastic bag presses directly onto the gum for 15–20 min. Once brittle, it fractures and lifts out of the pile. Solvent (Goo Gone / IPA) cleans residue. Water does nothing at any temp. Cotton / synthetic carpet tolerates IPA, POG, NSD at room temp. Wool carpet narrows the lane slightly.
whyThisWorks: Ice-brittle + pick out is 80%+ of the removal. Residue handled by solvent tamp + Dawn. Weighted dry towel between any wet step protects the pad.
defaultAssumption: Most gum on carpet cases are kid-related or foot-traffic. Freeze-first with an ice pack avoids any water near the pad.

spottingProtocol.technique: Press ice pack on gum, not the carpet
spottingProtocol.equipment: Ice pack in a plastic bag, OR ice cubes in a zip-top bag
spottingProtocol.instruction: Press an ice pack (wrapped in plastic — no melt into carpet) directly on the gum for 15–20 min. Gum goes brittle. Do NOT let ice melt into the carpet.
Immediately after chilling, pry the brittle gum off with a dull blade. Flakes and chunks lift out. Work fast — it re-softens within a few minutes.

spottingProtocol.agent: Goo Gone or IPA on White Cloth
spottingProtocol.equipment: Goo Gone OR IPA on clean white cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Tamp residual pigment / softener residue with solvent on a cloth. Never pour on carpet. Flip cloth frequently.
NSD-damp cloth to clear solvent. Blot with dry towel.
Fan-dry the area. Once dry, vacuum to lift any loose particles.

steps: 1. Ice pack direct on gum 15–20 min — brittle-freeze.
3. Goo Gone / IPA on white cloth for residue.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold (ice) for mechanical phase; room-temp solvent
warnings: Never let ice melt into carpet
Never skip the freeze step — solvent on warm gum = bigger mess

homeSolutions.instruction: Grab an ice pack or a ziplock bag filled with ice cubes. Wrap it in a plastic bag so water can't drip onto the carpet. Press it directly on the gum for 15–20 minutes. Frozen gum is brittle and lifts out of the pile cleanly; warm gum smears deeper in.

homeSolutions.agent: Pick and Scrape Right After Freezing
homeSolutions.instruction: Pry the brittle gum out of the carpet with a dull butter knife or credit-card edge. Flakes and chunks come free. Work fast — you have a few minutes before it re-softens.

homeSolutions.agent: Goo Gone or Rubbing Alcohol on a White Cloth
homeSolutions.instruction: For any colored / oily residue: dab Goo Gone or 70%+ rubbing alcohol on a clean white cloth, press onto the spot. Flip cloth frequently. Never pour solvent directly on carpet — it sinks to the pad.
Dampen a clean cloth with warm water + a drop of Dawn. Press on the spot to clear solvent residue. Blot with a dry towel.
Point a fan at the spot. Once the carpet is bone-dry, vacuum over the area to lift any loose residual particles.

steps: 1. Ice pack (in plastic bag) direct on gum, 15–20 min.
2. Scrape brittle gum off with a dull blade.
3. Goo Gone or rubbing alcohol on a white cloth for residue.
4. Dawn damp cloth to clear solvent.
products: Dull butter knife or credit-card edge
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Very rare to need a pro for gum on carpet — the freeze + scrape handles most cases.
warnings: Don't let ice melt onto carpet
Ventilate when using Goo Gone / alcohol

neverDo: Never let ice melt into carpet
Never rub warm gum into the pile
fiberSensitivities: Nylon / olefin / polyester / wool: all tolerate freeze + light solvent tamp

materialWarnings: Ice pack in PLASTIC — no melt into carpet

escalation.when: Rare. Only if the gum melted through a shampooer / steam cleaner cycle and pressed into backing, or residual pigment survives two solvent passes.
escalation.whatToTell: "Chewing gum on [nylon / wool / synthetic] carpet, [fresh / walked-in], home-treated with ice-pack freeze + scrape + Goo Gone + Dawn."

contextQuestions: Carpet fiber — nylon / olefin / polyester / wool?
Fresh gum or walked-in / sugar-crusted old?
Has any water / shampooer been applied?
Is this a high-pile or low-pile carpet?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Freeze-first-with-ice-pack is essentially the whole protocol. Chemistry is residue cleanup only.
